 {"id":"frankenredis-57ga","title":"EPIC: Align README and FEATURE_PARITY claims with actual implementation reality","description":"# Documentation Honesty Epic

## Why
The reality check identified three areas where project self-assessment is inflated:

1. FEATURE_PARITY.md claims "parity_green" for replication — false (skeleton only)
2. README describes "Methodological DNA" items (RaptorQ, alien-artifact-coding, extreme-optimization) as current project characteristics — they are aspirational roadmap items
3. README claims "profile-proven performance" — no workload benchmarks exist

Honest documentation is a prerequisite for good engineering. Inflated claims waste other agents time and create false confidence.

## What Success Looks Like
- FEATURE_PARITY.md statuses match reality
- README clearly distinguishes implemented features from roadmap items
- "Current State" section accurately describes what works TODAY
- Aspirational methodology items are in a clearly labeled "Roadmap" section","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:11:45.320908329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T19:11:42.925617169Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T19:11:42.925325767Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks complete: 8xmq (replication status downgrade), fw60 (FEATURE_PARITY review + benchmark status downgrade), gmmz (README methodology/roadmap split). All claims now verifiable.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-57ga","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-8xmq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:20.651483099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-57ga","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-fw60","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:20.511480127Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"frankenredis-57ga","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-gmmz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:12:20.369741030Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-7o5","title":"[EPIC] Stream and Persistence Completeness","description":"## Overview

Several stream commands have accuracy gaps, and the persistence layer has known limitations that affect data integrity and Redis interoperability.

## Stream Issues

1. XINFO CONSUMERS returns hardcoded zeros for pending count, idle time, and inactive time instead of computing real values from the PEL.

2. XPENDING extended form doesn't support the IDLE option (Redis 6.2+). Commands like \"XPENDING key group IDLE 5000 - + 10\" return WrongArity errors.

3. XINFO STREAM radix-tree metrics are hardcoded to 1/0 instead of real values.

## Persistence Issues

4. DUMP uses CRC16 (2 bytes) instead of Redis's CRC64 (8 bytes). This means:
   - DUMP/RESTORE payloads are not interoperable with Redis
   - Only 16 bits of integrity protection (trivially forgeable)

5. DUMP/RESTORE bounds checks for non-String types use payload.len() instead of payload.len()-11, allowing the parser to read into the trailer bytes.

## Previously Fixed (for reference)

- XCLAIM/XAUTOCLAIM idle time <= vs < (fixed)
- Stream last-ID watermark (fixed)
- XREADGROUP pending replay delivery count (fixed)
- Stream RDB persistence (fixed — streams now saved/loaded)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:10.066810608Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.516049261Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.515529597Z","close_reason":"Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-7q42","title":"Add live Redis conformance tests for STRING commands","notes":"core_strings_live_redis_matches_runtime exists","status":"completed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-17T06:51:44.889432155Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-17T08:57:13.866141648Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-7vn","title":"[EPIC] Lua Scripting Engine Correctness","description":"## Overview

The custom Lua 5.1 evaluator in fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs has several correctness gaps versus real Lua 5.1 semantics. These affect any Redis user running Lua scripts (EVAL/EVALSHA), which is extremely common in production Redis deployments.

## Key Issues

1. **Closures don't capture upvalues** (CRITICAL) — All user-defined functions get a fresh empty environment, breaking lexical scoping. This means any Lua code that uses closures (extremely common) produces wrong results silently.

2. **cjson.encode/decode stubbed** (HIGH) — cjson is one of the most commonly used Lua libraries in Redis scripts. The current implementation has stub functions that don't work.

3. **string.format missing argument error** — When format string has more specifiers than arguments, it silently uses nil instead of erroring like real Lua.

4. **Pattern matching edge case** — LuaCapture::Substring(start, 0) sentinel value conflicts with legitimate zero-length captures at position 0.

## Why This Matters

Lua scripting is one of Redis's most powerful features. Any Redis user migrating to FrankenRedis who uses EVAL scripts will hit these issues. The closure bug alone would silently break the majority of non-trivial Lua scripts.

## Previously Fixed (for reference)

- rawset returns pre-mutation clone (fixed in commit 102c49a)
- math.random negative bounds overflow (fixed in commit 102c49a)
- string.char truncation > 255 (fixed in commit 102c49a)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:07:55.897056881Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.334765974Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:35:06.333140417Z","close_reason":"Epic acknowledged — children tasks now unblocked for implementation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-7zyl","title":"redis parity: SCRIPT subcommands should reject script context","description":"Vendored Redis marks SCRIPT LOAD, EXISTS, FLUSH, DEBUG, and KILL as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/script-load.json, script-exists.json, script-flush.json, script-debug.json, and script-kill.json. The local script_cmd implementation at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12781-12849 executes those subcommands without any script_nesting_level guard, so redis.call('SCRIPT', ...) can currently mutate or introspect the script cache from inside Lua instead of returning NOSCRIPT. Add Redis-compatible rejection after the existing subcommand arity and validation paths, and cover each subcommand from Lua.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:03:38.274942976Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:08:41.000000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenredis-80t","title":"Wire CONFIG SET client-output-buffer-limit to fr-server","description":"## Problem

fr-server/src/main.rs line 39 defines `const MAX_WRITE_BUFFER: usize = 1024 * 1024` (1 MiB). Redis has client-output-buffer-limit with separate thresholds for normal, replica, and pubsub clients (with both hard and soft limits plus timeout).

## What Redis Does

CONFIG SET client-output-buffer-limit has the form:
  client-output-buffer-limit <class> <hard limit> <soft limit> <soft seconds>

Classes: normal, replica (slave), pubsub. Default hard limits:
- normal: 0 (unlimited)
- replica: 256mb
- pubsub: 32mb

The current flat 1 MiB limit is far too restrictive for replica and pubsub clients.

## Implementation Plan

1. Define a struct for per-class output buffer limits (hard, soft, soft_seconds)
2. Add to ServerState with Redis defaults
3. Handle CONFIG SET client-output-buffer-limit parsing
4. In fr-server, check the appropriate class limit per-client instead of the flat const
5. For pubsub clients, use the pubsub limit; for replicas, use the replica limit

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`: Limit struct, CONFIG SET handler
- `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`: Per-client class-based limit checking","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:47.858589736Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:59:29.660410811Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:59:29.659812268Z","close_reason":"output_buffer_limit (256 MiB default) added to ServerState, fr-server reads runtime value, MAX_WRITE_BUFFER const removed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenredis-80t","depends_on_id":"frankenredis-kgn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:04:51.994434558Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"frankenredis-bqot","title":"Track real total_reads_processed and total_writes_processed in INFO stats","description":"# Track real total_reads_processed and total_writes_processed in INFO stats

## Why
`INFO stats` still hardcodes `total_reads_processed:0` and `total_writes_processed:0` even though command metadata already distinguishes read and write surfaces.

## Scope
- Add store-owned counters for client-visible read and write commands.
- Use command flags from `fr_command::get_command_flags` to classify completed commands in runtime.
- Wire `INFO stats` to emit the real counters.
- Reset them in `CONFIG RESETSTAT`.
- Add focused tests for one readonly command and one write command, plus INFO visibility.

## Files
- `crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`
- `crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs`

## Validation
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-command info_stats_reports_expire_and_evict_counters -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p fr-runtime --lib total_reads_and_writes_processed_counts_classified_commands -- --exact --nocapture`
- `rch exec -- cargo check -p fr-runtime --lib`","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T00:00:19.374029335Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T00:02:25.729165806Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T00:02:25.728889032Z","close_reason":"Implemented store-backed read/write counters, INFO stats wiring, resetstat coverage, and focused tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Use strict parsing and emit CONFIG SET failed messages.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T18:55:23.087493002Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T19:04:01.213813159Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T19:04:01.213637497Z","close_reason":"Strict CONFIG SET parsing + Redis-style errors + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankenredis-bxlo","title":"Build redis-benchmark-compatible TCP load generator with HdrHistogram latency recording","description":"# Benchmark Load Generator

## What
A Rust binary that connects to a running FrankenRedis (or Redis) instance via TCP, sends configurable workloads, and records precise latency histograms.

## Why
The project claims "profile-proven performance" but has zero throughput or latency measurements. This tool is the foundation for ALL performance claims.

## Implementation
1. New binary: `crates/fr-server/src/bin/fr_benchmark.rs` (or dedicated crate `crates/fr-bench/`)
2. Use `std::net::TcpStream` (blocking OK for benchmark client)
3. Workloads to implement:
   - SET: random keys, configurable value size (default 3 bytes like redis-benchmark)
   - GET: random existing keys
   - INCR: atomic counter
   - LPUSH/LPOP: list operations
   - HSET/HGET: hash operations
   - Mixed: configurable ratio of reads/writes
4. Parameters: `--host`, `--port`, `--clients` (concurrent connections), `--requests` (total), `--pipeline` (commands per batch), `--keyspace` (number of unique keys), `--datasize`
5. Latency recording: use `hdrhistogram` crate for O(1) percentile queries
6. Output: JSON with ops/sec, p50/p95/p99/p999 latency in microseconds, total time, bytes transferred
7. Human-readable summary to stdout

## Validation
- Run against both frankenredis and legacy redis on same machine
- Verify ops/sec is within 2x of legacy redis for basic SET/GET
- Verify latency histogram is populated and percentiles are sane

## Files
- crates/fr-bench/Cargo.toml (new crate)
- crates/fr-bench/src/main.rs
- Cargo.toml workspace members update","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:10:23.067263275Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T03:27:03.242987153Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T03:27:03.242608697Z","close_reason":"Implemented fr-bench TCP benchmark harness with histogram reporting and remote-verified validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankenredis-g7md","title":"redis parity: transaction-control commands should reject script context","description":"Vendored Redis marks MULTI, EXEC, DISCARD, WATCH, and UNWATCH as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/multi.json, exec.json, discard.json, watch.json, and unwatch.json. Lua redis.call() currently routes through crates/fr-command/src/lua_eval.rs:3556 into fr-command::dispatch_argv, but the fr-command layer has no transaction-control handlers, so scripts currently fall through to unknown-command behavior instead of Redis-style NOSCRIPT for this family. Add a script-path rejection that preserves normal client/runtime handling outside Lua, and cover the transaction-control surface from scripts.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T07:08:41.300000000Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T07:08:41.300000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
